content: Cover page of 'Annual Subway Registrations 1940 to 1995' report, prepared by Office of Management and Budget, New York City Transit, October 1996. Contains two black and white photographs of NYC subway cars and no tabular data.

Introduction/preface page for 'Annual Subway Registrations 1940 to 1995' report. Contains descriptive text explaining the report's scope, methodology, terminology (registrations, Conductor's Collections), station groupings by sector (Upper Manhattan/Bronx, Northern Queens, Brooklyn/Southern Queens, Manhattan CBD), and notes about fare collection policies. No tabular data present. Page numbered 'i'.

Narrative text page (page ii) describing historical changes to the New York City subway system since 1940, including line closures, new line openings, station closures, and notes about system mileage and station counts. No tabular data present.

Page iii of a notes/preface section containing explanatory text about the transit data report. Discusses transfer points, station listings methodology, miscellaneous adjustments category, and contact information for John Kennes at the Office of Management and Budget, Revenue Analysis Division, 130 Livingston Street, Brooklyn, NY 11201. No tabular data present.

Table of contents page for 'Annual Station Registrations 1940 through 1995' document. Lists sections: Northern Manhattan and Bronx Registrations (pages 1, 9, 17), Northern Queens Registrations (page 25), Southern Queens and Brooklyn Registrations (pages 33, 41, 49, 57), and Manhattan CBD Registrations (pages 65, 73). No tabular ridership data present.

page_description: New York City Subway Fare Chronology 1940 to 1995 - listing fare dates, amounts, and notable changes

content: Page vi of a historical NYC transit report showing a line graph titled 'Annual Subway Registrations 1940 to 1995' with ridership in billions on the y-axis and years on the x-axis, accompanied by explanatory text describing ridership trends over the period. No tabular data is present.
